data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Autodesk sets Q2 guidance above analysts estimates
Cal Redwood Acquisition prices 20M units in IPO
data.description:
• Cal Redwood Acquisition (CRA) announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 20M units at $10/unit on May 22, 2025.
  • The units are expected to be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “CRAQU” beginning May 23, 2025.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one right to receive one tenth of a Class A ordinary share upon the consummation of an initial business combination.
  • Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Company expects that its Class A ordinary shares and rights will be listed on Nasdaq under the symbols “CRA” and “CRAQR,” respectively.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 27, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Onconetix receives additional notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Onconetix (NASDAQ:ONCO) said on Thursday that it has received a delisting letter from Nasdaq, indicating that the company’s failure to file its quarterly report is in violation of its continued listing requirements. 
  • The company has already received a deficiency notice from Nasdaq that the company was not in compliance, given its failure to timely file its annual report. 
  • The company intends to file the Form 10-K and 10-Q as promptly as possible to regain compliance.

data.title: VCI Global files to sell 55.26M ordinary shares for holders

data.title: Soluna Holdings launches equity and warrant offering
data.description:
• Soluna Holdings (NASDAQ:SLNH) is offering shares of its common stock along with Series A and Series B warrants in a public offering on Nasdaq.
  • Each common share will be sold together with one Series A Warrant and one Series B Warrant.
  • Series A Warrants will expire five years after their initial exercisability, while Series B Warrants will expire after two years.
  • The company is also issuing pre-funded warrants for investors seeking to avoid exceeding 4.99% or 9.99% ownership thresholds.
  • Each pre-funded warrant will be exercisable immediately and sold with the same accompanying common warrants as the common stock.
  • SLNH shares down 1.3% post-market on Thursday.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Ralph Lauren outlines low single-digit revenue growth for fiscal 2026 while expanding DTC and international focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ralph Lauren Corporation (RL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Patrice Louvet, President and CEO, emphasized delivering on both strategic and financial commitments, noting "our achievements this fiscal year embodied so much of what Ralph Lauren's stands for as one of the most beloved iconic brands in the world." He highlighted 8% top line growth for the full year and record revenues in international businesses, with Europe and Asia now comprising the majority of company revenues. Louvet also underscored the company's resilience amid global volatility and outlined ongoing investments in brand desirability, market share, and technology to support future growth.

  • Louvet described broad-based growth in the fourth quarter, with "continued progress across our three long-term strategic pillars," and stressed the success of marketing activations, new customer acquisition, and the strength of core product sales. He pointed to growth in high-potential categories—women's apparel, outerwear, and handbags—and direct-to-consumer (DTC) channels, as well as the opening of 83 new stores globally, primarily in Asia.

  • Justin Picicci, CFO, stated, "We closed out the year with fourth quarter results that exceeded our expectations across revenues and gross and operating margins, all three regions contributed to both revenue growth and operating margin expansion." Picicci highlighted $1 billion in free cash flow, $625 million returned to shareholders, and a new 10% increase in the annual dividend with an additional $1.5 billion in share repurchases. He noted "total company fourth quarter revenue growth of 10% was above our 6% to 7% outlook," and gross margin expansion to 69.2%, driven by AUR growth and favorable mix.

Outlook

  • Picicci reported, "For fiscal '26, we expect constant currency revenues to increase low single-digits, driven by our growth in our Asia and Europe businesses." He added, "We expect this year's performance to be heavily weighted to the first half of the year, notably Q1, with first half revenues up roughly mid-single-digits."

  • Operating margin is expected to expand modestly in constant currency, with gross margins projected to be roughly flat to last year. The company is assessing further pricing actions to offset potential tariff impacts in the second half of fiscal '26.

  • The outlook reflects caution in North America due to macroeconomic indicators, tariffs, and weakening consumer confidence.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue growth reached 10%, exceeding the company's 6% to 7% outlook, with broad-based performance across direct-to-consumer and wholesale channels.

  • Europe led regional growth with sales increasing 16%, Asia rose 13%, and North America grew 6%. Total company comp sales increased 13%.

  • Adjusted gross margin expanded 260 basis points to 69.2%, supported by 9% AUR growth, reduced discounting, and favorable geographic mix. Operating margin expanded 240 basis points to 11.1%, with operating profit up 40%.

  • The company ended the year with $2.1 billion in cash and $1.1 billion in total debt. Inventory was up 5% year-over-year, aligned with global demand and an Easter timing shift.

  • The board authorized a 10% annual dividend increase and $1.5 billion in additional share repurchases.

Q&A

  • Matt Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about strategy adjustments amid consumer uncertainty and the bridge between Q1 and full-year revenue growth. Louvet responded that "the backbone of our strategy is as relevant today as it was a few years ago and will remain broadly unchanged," citing resilience and agility. Picicci explained that North America guidance is more cautious for the second half of the year due to macro factors.

  • Jay Sole, UBS: Inquired about pricing strategy under evolving tariffs and AUR growth. Picicci detailed a toolkit of supply chain diversification, cost efficiencies, and "selective pricing actions and further reductions in discounting," expecting AUR to remain high single-digits in Q1.

  • Michael Binetti, Evercore ISI: Asked about additional pricing for tariffs and revenue momentum by geography. Picicci confirmed proactive pricing for fall '25 with further actions being assessed and stated Asia will lead growth, followed by Europe, with North America expected to decline low to mid single-digits.

  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory Group: Questioned marketing spend, category performance, and real estate plans. Louvet said marketing would remain at 7.3% of revenue for the coming year and highlighted ongoing momentum in women's, outerwear, and handbags. Picicci discussed the strategic SoHo store purchase and selective real estate investments.

  • Ike Boruchow, Wells Fargo: Probed on US wholesale channel outlook. Picicci described stabilization and ongoing pruning of lower-tier department store doors, with a focus on top-tier distribution and digital traction.

  • John Kernan, TD Cowen: Sought details on Europe’s margin outlook and key city strategy. Picicci noted strong brand positioning and new store openings, while Louvet highlighted progress in expanding DTC presence.

  • Laurent Vasilescu, BNP Paribas: Asked about China’s performance and gross margin outlook. Louvet indicated China grew 20% in the quarter and is expected to maintain low double-digit growth. Picicci projected flat gross margins year-over-year, with stronger first-half performance.

  • Chris Nardone, Bank of America: Queried on long-term operating margin expansion and AUR potential. Picicci noted exceeding the 15% operating margin target and ongoing SG&A leverage, with AUR growth driven by product mix, reduced promotions, and channel strategy.

  • Tom Nikic, Needham: Asked about international consumer sentiment. Louvet reported no signs of anti-American sentiment or slowing international momentum.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, probing on macro headwinds, guidance, pricing strategies, and channel dynamics, with a neutral to slightly positive tone. Questions focused on the sustainability of growth, tariff impact, and regional outlooks.

  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in prepared remarks, with Louvet stating, "we are still encouraged by what we're seeing so far," but acknowledged macro uncertainties and the need for agility. In the Q&A, management’s tone was measured, emphasizing flexibility and a diversified approach.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed slightly increased caution regarding North America, tariffs, and the global outlook, though confidence in international growth and brand momentum remained strong.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from a 6% to 7% fiscal year revenue growth outlook in Q3 to a low single-digit projection for fiscal 2026, reflecting increased macro caution, particularly in North America.

  • Strategic focus on DTC, international expansion, and high-potential categories remained consistent, but management highlighted greater uncertainty due to tariffs and consumer sentiment.

  • Analysts continued to focus on marketing ROI, pricing power, and regional performance, with more attention to potential risks and mitigation strategies this quarter.

  • Management’s tone in Q4 was more prudent, especially regarding North American outlook, compared to the more upbeat Q3 call.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited macroeconomic pressures, tariff impacts, and weakening consumer confidence as primary risks for fiscal 2026.

  • Picicci outlined mitigation strategies including supply chain diversification, cost efficiencies, and selective pricing actions to address tariff headwinds.

  • Analysts repeatedly questioned the sustainability of pricing power, the impact of tariffs, and the ability to maintain growth in a more uncertain backdrop.

Final Takeaway

Ralph Lauren closed fiscal 2025 with strong international and DTC momentum and delivered on its strategic and financial commitments. Looking ahead to fiscal 2026, management expects low single-digit revenue growth, with performance weighted toward the first half of the year and ongoing caution in North America due to macroeconomic and tariff-related uncertainties. The company remains focused on brand elevation, expanding in key markets, and leveraging its diversified growth drivers and agile supply chain to navigate evolving challenges and support long-term value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Red River Bancshares launches $5.1M share buyback program
data.description:
• Red River Bancshares (NASDAQ:RRBI) said on Thursday that it has entered into a stock repurchase agreement with a shareholder to buyback 100,000 shares of its common stock for $5.1M.
  • The repurchase is supplemental to the company’s previously announced $5M repurchase program. As of May 22, the company had about $4.7M of remaining capacity under the repurchase program.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Franklin Templeton announces liquidation of three ETFs
data.description:
Investment manager Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN), better known as Franklin Templeton, announced on Thursday the liquidation of three exchange-traded funds.

The ETFs are Western Asset Short Duration Income ETF (NASDAQ:WINC), Western Asset Total Return ETF (NASDAQ:WBND), and Franklin FTSE Hong Kong ETF (NYSEARCA:FLHK).

The board of trustees approved the three liquidations on May 21.

WINC and WBND are set to be liquidated and dissolved on or about August 29.

Trading in the funds will halt before the market open on August 23. The proceeds are scheduled to be sent to shareholders on or about August 29.

FLHK is anticipated to be liquidated and dissolved on or about July 8.

Trading in the fund is set to halt prior to the market open on July 2. The proceeds are scheduled to be sent to shareholders on or about July 8.
